subject_line: ZBB, organisational design, and the PE pressure point in 2026
email_draft:
The ZBB work you led at [FIRM NAME] — specifically the application to portfolio company cost architecture rather than the firm itself — is not something many people in this market are doing with that level of operational specificity. It caught our attention.

[INSERT YOUR NETWORK CONNECTION HERE]

We have been watching how mid-market PE firms across the UK are navigating the post-consolidation talent question — specifically the point at which the integration brief outpaces the internal capability available to execute it. The firms we work with are increasingly treating this as a strategic timing question rather than a reactive one.

If that tension is one you are thinking about at [FIRM NAME], I would welcome a brief conversation.
edit_notes: Hook is anchored to the ZBB methodology attribution from Exa. Bridge references July 2025 UK wealth M&A consolidation signal from Perplexity. Network connection placeholder is on its own line — do not remove or write around it.

personalisation_guidance.recipient_values: Operational specificity and methodology rigour over generalist advisory. Peer-level recognition of technical depth rather than seniority flattery.
personalisation_guidance.strongest_element: The ZBB hook is highly specific and non-obvious — it reads as something only someone paying close attention to their work would know.
personalisation_guidance.risk_or_limitation: Credibility anchor is drawn from a single Exa source. If the ZBB attribution is inaccurate or has since changed context, the hook loses its specificity. Consultant should verify before sending.
personalisation_guidance.network_connection_framing: Strongest if the network connection references someone who has worked directly with [CONTACT NAME] on a portfolio engagement rather than a general sector contact.
